outcome_contract.why: The #new-ideas and #in-progress sections need to be separate pages so each can be focused, navigated to directly, and not compete for vertical space.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: #new-ideas and #in-progress each live at their own URL; navigation links point to each; root /process-improvements redirects or shows a landing.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Hero anchor links become `<Link>` to new routes; sub-nav added via `layout.tsx`
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Sub-nav rendered by `layout.tsx`; hero tiles become `<Link href="/process-improvements/...">`

engineering_coverage.applicable: Each page has independent filter state, pendingState, bulkSelection
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Shared state still initialised; conditionals guard unused state
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Independent state — no cross-section mutation risk

engineering_coverage.applicable: Tests follow component — `InProgressInbox.test.tsx` and `NewIdeasInbox.test.tsx` split from monolith tests
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Test must deal with mode prop; existing fixture patterns still work
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Portable — RTL render pattern is identical; same `initialXxx` props, different component import

engineering_coverage.applicable: Each `page.tsx` calls only the data functions relevant to its section; auto-refresh continues unchanged
Marginally faster page loads (each page fetches less)

open_operator_questions: Should the business filter dropdown appear on the In Progress page?

stage_payload.decision_summary: The `/process-improvements` page must be split so that In Progress and New Ideas each have their own URL. The core decision is how to decompose the monolithic `ProcessImprovementsInbox` client component (~1,750 lines) that currently owns all state and renders for both sections. The data layer needs no changes — the question is purely about component structure and routing.
goals: `/process-improvements/in-progress` — focused page for active plan monitoring
`/process-improvements/new-ideas` — focused page for queue triage (WorkItems + Deferred + Recently Actioned)
Navigation updated to link to both sub-pages
non_goals: Changing API routes or data model
Adding new features during the split
constraints: No new API routes — existing `/api/process-improvements/items` and decision routes sufficient
Existing `ProcessImprovementsInbox.test.tsx` tests must pass after split
assumptions: Deferred and Recently Actioned sections stay with New Ideas
BulkActionBar stays with New Ideas (queue items only)
Business filter retained on both pages (default; operator can remove from In Progress later)

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Existing test suite must pass without wholesale rewrite
Each page must own its own state independently
Monolith is large but sections are already logically scoped
Internal tool; revert PR must fully restore current behaviour

options_considered.Description: Extract `InProgressInbox` and `NewIdeasInbox` as two independent client components, each with their own `page.tsx`. Root route redirects. Nav gets two links or a sub-nav via `layout.tsx`.
options_considered.Upside: Matches existing pattern exactly; clean state isolation; data fetching per page; test suites independent
options_considered.Downside: More files to create; ~1,750-line monolith must be read carefully during decomposition
options_considered.Key risks: Missing a shared utility that both components need (unlikely given fact-find; both sections use only section-specific sub-components)

options_considered.Upside: "new-ideas"` prop to `ProcessImprovementsInbox`; render only the relevant section; two new `page.tsx` files pass the prop
options_considered.Downside: Fewer new files; monolith stays in one place
options_considered.Key risks: All state still initialised regardless of mode; conditional rendering adds coupling; test isolation is superficial; root data fetch remains monolithic; doesn't actually achieve clean page-level separation
options_considered.Viable?: State for unused section is initialised and polled; harder to prune later; only looks cheaper on the surface

options_considered.Option: C — Shared layout with tabs (no URL split)
options_considered.Description: Add a `layout.tsx` with tabs, keep URL as `/process-improvements?tab=...`
options_considered.Downside: Does not meet the goal — operator explicitly wants separate URLs

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option A — Full extraction into two independent client components, each with their own `page.tsx` sub-route. Root `/process-improvements` redirects to `/process-improvements/new-ideas`. Sub-nav rendered by a shared `layout.tsx` inside the `process-improvements/` directory.
chosen_approach.Why this wins: It matches the existing `page.tsx` + client-component pattern used throughout BOS. State isolation is real, not cosmetic. Data fetching per page is more precise. Tests follow the component and remain fully independent. The decomposition is mechanical — all private sub-components in the monolith are already logically scoped to one section.
chosen_approach.What it depends on: The monolith's private sub-components (`InProgressSection`, `ActivePlanCard`, `InboxSection`, `WorkItemCard`, `BulkActionBar`, `RecentlyActionedSection`, `ProcessImprovementsSummary`) being extractable without coupling; the fact-find confirms this (no cross-section sub-component usage found).
chosen_approach.Q: Should the business filter dropdown appear on the In Progress page?
chosen_approach.Why operator input is required: UX preference; the filter is trivial to include or exclude technically.
chosen_approach.Planning impact: affects which filter UI elements are included in `InProgressInbox`; default assumption is to include it (mirrors current behaviour).

rejected_approaches: Option B (parameterised monolith) — superficially cheaper but keeps all state initialised for both sections, adds conditional logic, and doesn't achieve clean URL-level separation. Deferred cost, not saved cost.
Option C (tabs, no URL split) — fails the stated goal of separate URLs.

validation_implications: Existing `ProcessImprovementsInbox.test.tsx` must be split across `InProgressInbox.test.tsx` and `NewIdeasInbox.test.tsx` covering the same TCs
Hero tile link update in `page.tsx` must use Next.js `<Link>` (not `<a>`)
Root `page.tsx` redirect must use `import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'`
sequencing_constraints: TASK-01 (layout + redirect) can be done first or last — it is independent of component decomposition
TASK-02 and TASK-03 can be done in either order but must not be parallelised (both touch the monolith file)
risks_to_carry_forward: `InProgressSection` renders `null` when `activePlans.length === 0` — needs an empty state on its own page
Hero tiles on each sub-page should cross-link to the sibling page (not self-link); planner must confirm which tiles appear on which page

areas.label: Page routing
areas.trigger: Operator visits URL or clicks nav

areas.current: Part of `ProcessImprovementsInbox`, shares state with New Ideas
